transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Hey, Anjana, how's it going?
Sorry, you're on mute.
Hi, good.
How are you?
Yeah, I'm doing great.
I'm out of my home office in Houston and in the growthx office in San
San Francisco, so I'm having quite a good time this week.
Nice.
I didn't know you.
Like, I thought you were fully remote.
Yeah, I usually am, but I get to come up to this office maybe like once every one or two months.
So do you ever find yourself in San Francisco?
I'll let you know, for sure.
What's that?
No, I'll definitely check in if I'm in San Francisco.
Would love to, like, get to know people at Growthx, too.
Yeah, definitely.
We host all these events, and we're doing, like, a breakfast for marketing leaders tomorrow.
So if you're ever in San Francisco, let us know, and you're always free to come work from our office.
Oh, I would love to.
Yeah, where do I find out about these events?
Yeah, I'll shoot you, like, I think we're, like, building out a homepage for it, but I'll just, like, send you links as we have new events that come up.
Yeah, that sounds great.
Yeah, thank you.
Um, I think Dora might be late, but I'm...
I'm to check, or we can get started either way, because this is my project.
Hold on.
Yeah, and I don't think she was on the invite, so I think it's just us, unless you added her.
Oh, sorry, I thought she was on the invite.
Okay, never mind.
But that's okay.
mean, today the goal is really just like for us to start preparing ahead of the kickoff, and I was also happy to answer any questions that you had sort of heading into this meeting.
Oh, sorry, give me a second.
She just responded, but yes.
Oh, yeah, take your time.
Sorry.
Yeah, she might join us now.
Perfect.
Perfect.
But again, did you have any questions just like to sort of kick off this convo?
Yeah.
Yes.
So, okay, she's in a budget.
We can just get started either way.
So, yes, we...
I have approval to most likely move forward.
That's the plan.
What I need to know is the timeline and we definitely, like I have to loop finance and I quickly saw the scope of work there reviewing it.
Only thing is I know there'll be some negotiation involved.
So we would need to kind of revisit the terms a little bit.
I'm just giving you like a transparent heads up there.
So they will be in touch.
But yeah, the timeline, you can let me know.
And just generally, I know there's a two-month period for strategy and then you hit the ground running.
Is there a way to speed things up there?
Sorry.
What was the last part of what you said?
I missed the question.
Is there a way to speed things up?
Yeah.
So a good way to think about it.
And I'm just going to kind of pull up a quick document that I have here.
So the first goal is to create the artifacts right around your company, your audience, your brand.
Because that'll get the context engineering we need to make the workflows really relevant.
So we'll do that in like week one.
We'll get that done right away.
We'll also like ingest resources from you.
I'll send you a list of the resources so that we don't have to like meet with you to start making the artifacts.
Although we'll wait until after our kickoff call because we want the nuance from the conversation to like inform the way that they're created.
We'll also get access to Google Search Console, Google Analytics, and your CMS so that we can do all the reporting and analytics for you on the content and also so that we can stage and publish.
And then we'll set up our content OS, which is the roadmap of assignments and topic clusters that we would focus on in like week three with the first calibration article done in week four.
And so after week four, if we can get all these things done by collaborating, we'll actually move towards staging and publishing articles.
And we'll try to get at least 10 done by the end of the strategy sprint.
So by the end of that strategy sprint, you should have 10 published articles in your CMS, if not more.
Okay, that sounds great.
Yeah, then we'll start the metric performance tracking usually starts at the following month, I'm guessing?
Exactly.
Like, ahead of the 12-month growth execution, none of our clients have, right, all of the data around performance before they're making that decision about the 12-month term, right?
They just understand, one, the quality that we can hit, two, the assignments that we would focus, the roadmap that we would execute on.
And then, you know, they're taking a leap of faith a bit, but we've got all these success stories we can point to as well.
So, yeah, you wouldn't be able to make that decision about phase two with all this data around, like, performance and traffic growth.
Because that's impossible to do in a month, right?
Yeah.
No, I know.
That's kind of why I was asking.
So is there flexibility?
Like we would need it on the 12-month period because I also saw that the contract is up front.
So what is the type of flexibility you can offer us there?
We can definitely do like a termination for breach if you feel like we're not sticking to anything that we commit to contractually.
But we can't do less than a 12-month term for phase two because we give you the ability to opt out right by the end of phase one.
So right now, every client we work with has a 12-month phase two.
So is that a monthly payment?
We can definitely be flexible on payment terms.
So we typically just, you know, wait to see what we get from the red lines and then negotiate from there.
But we could look at quarterly for sure.
And if monthly was like an absolute must, and that was like the only sticking point, then we could talk internally about monthly.
But that's possible for sure.
Can you also give me options if there's like a price tier, just a lower price, what that scope might look like?
So I'm weighing the trade-offs and I'm going to go to them with multiple options.
So I just want to know what I can work with so we can move quickly.
Yeah, for sure.
I guess like what are some of the other options that you're considering?
Are they like AI-forward agencies?
Are they software platforms?
Well, you are at the top of the list.
So growthx is at the top.
What I'm looking for is for me to make the decision.
It would depend on the price and the terms.
You seem to be flexible on the monthly or even quarterly.
It depends.
I can maybe talk to finance too.
I can't promise anything right now, but ideally, like I will try to make that work too.
But I need flexibility on the price as well.
So, you know, finance will get in touch anyway, but if you and I can align, it'll just speed things up.
That's kind of where I'm coming from.
Yeah, that makes total sense.
I think we just need like a little bit of direction to start that conversation off because we typically are signing every client to that 15K for phase two.
And there's been some rare exceptions when we've gone from 15 to 12.
And I guess like we would need to know, one, rank, is that going to solve the problem, getting to 12 in phase two?
And then two, like just being super aligned on what our scope is, what your expectations are.
If there is someone who would be pushing back on the price, can we have a chance to talk to them about like the value we add or about the success stories that we've had with different clients?
Things like that.
Do you think if we went from 15 to 12 on phase two, would that give you what you need or would there need to be more for this to work?
That's
That might, yeah.
Another option, I'm just getting all of this.
Yeah, that might work.
Thank you so much.
The other question I have is, can we make the 12-month, because right now we've combined the scope of work, right, the strategy and the 12-month commitment.
Can we make the 12-month commitment after the strategy phase?

We wouldn't be able to do that just because we, like, only do kick two kickoffs a week, and in some situations we do three, and we are planning, like, our resource engagement, our staffing model, several weeks in advance.

Yeah.

But what we can do is we can have articles published, we can have the roadmap in your hands by, like, week three, week four, and you can see everything about what you would expect in phase two before.

It's eight weeks where there's that platform for the strategy sprint, and then it moves into a 12-month.
After that, right?
Okay, that's just about that.
terms that we agreed to correct, exactly.

We're going to move that to our own platform, which we'll have access to.
you.

you remember which one that was?

go.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Finalize terms for the GrowthX partnership.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=138.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Deal is approved, pending negotiation.** Anjana needs flexibility on the 12-month contract term and price to secure finance approval.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=178.0)
  - [**GrowthX offered a price reduction** from $15k to $12k/mo for the 12-month execution phase, plus flexible quarterly or monthly payment terms.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=433.0)
  - [**Parloa can save on its Profound subscription** by using GrowthX's new, integrated reporting platform, which replaces their current Scrunch partner tool.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=654.0)
  - [**An earlier kickoff (early Jan)** is possible if Parloa provides contract redlines quickly, before Anjana's OOO period (last two weeks of Dec).](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=1386.0)

## Topics

### Partnership Terms & Negotiation

  - [**Approval Status:** Deal is approved, pending final negotiation with Parloa's finance team.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=178.0)
  - [**Key Negotiation Points:**](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=182.0)
      - [**Contract Term:** Anjana requested flexibility on the 12-month execution phase.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=545.0)
          - [**GrowthX Rationale:** The 12-month term is non-negotiable. It's required for resource planning and is balanced by an opt-out clause at the end of the 8-week strategy sprint.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=545.0)
      - [**Price:** Anjana requested a lower-priced scope to present multiple options to finance.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=433.0)
          - [**GrowthX Offer:** A potential reduction from $15k to $12k/mo for the 12-month execution phase.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=491.0)
      - [**Payment Terms:** Anjana requested flexibility on the upfront payment structure.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=367.0)
          - [**GrowthX Offer:** Quarterly payments are available; monthly payments are possible if it's the sole remaining blocker.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=404.0)

### Strategy & Execution Timeline

  - [**Goal:** Accelerate the 8-week strategy sprint.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=204.0)
  - [**Timeline:**](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=221.0)
      - [**Week 1:** Ingest resources (post-kickoff) → Create company/audience artifacts.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=221.0)
      - [**Week 2:** Gain access to GSC, GA, & CMS.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=266.0)
      - [**Week 3:** Finalize Content OS (roadmap & topic clusters).](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=279.0)
      - [**Week 4:** Deliver first calibration article.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=279.0)
      - [**End of Sprint:** Publish ≥10 articles.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=290.0)
  - [**Rationale:** This accelerated timeline provides a concrete deliverable (published articles) for Parloa's opt-out decision, as performance data is not available in just 8 weeks.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=312.0)

### Content Strategy & Performance Goals

  - [**Audience:** Enterprise CX buyers (primary); Product/CTO (secondary).](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=951.0)
  - [**Content Focus:**](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=951.0)
      - [**GrowthX:** Top-of-funnel content (e.g., NPS guides, glossaries) to improve website performance.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=1120.0)
      - [**Parloa:** Technical content (e.g., product announcements) will remain in-house.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=876.0)
  - [**Review Process:** Anjana is the sole reviewer to prevent bottlenecks. The goal is to reach a point of minimal review before direct publishing.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=775.0)
  - [**Performance Metrics:**](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=1165.0)
      - [**Primary:** Sessions & Total Users (unique visitors).](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=1165.0)
      - [**Secondary:** Leads (demo requests, downloads).](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=1165.0)
      - [**Key Goal:** 4x total traffic, with a focus on US growth.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=1201.0)

### Reporting & Tooling

  - [**Profound Replacement:** GrowthX's new reporting platform can replace Parloa's Profound subscription, offering a potential cost saving.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=654.0)
  - [**Attribution:** GrowthX will use Parloa's existing first/last-touch attribution model and will not build a new one.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=1272.0)
  - [**Reporting Dashboard:** GrowthX will build a Looker dashboard using Parloa's existing setup.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=1293.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Anjana:**](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=1481.0)
      - [Nudge finance team to send contract redlines by Dec 4.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=1481.0)
      - [Confirm the Profound contract's opt-out date.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=733.0)
      - [Share 2–3 ideal content examples.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=775.0)
  - [**Golden Gate:**](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=1331.0)
      - [Share an example of a GrowthX reporting dashboard.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=1316.0)
      - [Provide a demo video of the new reporting platform.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=699.0)
      - [Investigate an earlier kickoff (early Jan) upon receipt of redlines.](https://fathom.video/share/Newxwg6zDRrM2UFcGaG3esydPBqeTMhz?tab=summary&timestamp=1386.0)
